key_verified: PIDM does not cover e-wallet balances (VERIFIED m_true 0.9)
RM2 billion+ subsidies since 2020 including eTunai/ePenjana/eStart/ePemula/eTunai Belia/eMADANI (VERIFIED)
BNM revised e-money policy effective 31 Jan 2025 with trust account requirements (VERIFIED)
53,000 under-30 carry RM1.9B debt (VERIFIED via AKPK)
Consumer Credit Act 2025 in force 1 March 2026, Part V 1 June 2026 (VERIFIED)
FMOS operational since Jan 2025, launched 17 Jan 2025 (VERIFIED)
key_incorrect: INCORRECT: 'No minimum capital adequacy' — BNM DOES impose minimum capital funds requirement, earlier policy referenced 8% of outstanding e-money liabilities benchmark. Must correct.
INCORRECT: 'eMADANI excluded those without 2022 tax filing history' — Actual criteria were STR recipients or income ≤RM100K based on IRB records as of 15 July 2023. Must correct.
INCORRECT: 'India UPI routes all wallet transactions through insured bank accounts' — UPI has PPI interoperability, not blanket insurance routing. Must remove or reword.
MISLEADING: '4.1M elderly / 12% per DOSM 2025' — DOSM 2025 shows 8.0% aged 65+. The 12% may refer to 60+ but needs re-sourcing.
MISLEADING: 'Singapore MAS mandates 100% liquid assets' — MAS requires safeguarding under Payment Services Act but not verified exact wording.
UNVERIFIED: Kenya M-Pesa pass-through insurance claim — should remove.
key_misleading: MISLEADING: RM6.7M/RM2.77B = 0.24% or 1.2% recovery rate — sources link RM34M/RM6.7M/RM17.5M to narrower scam-recovery pipeline, not the full RM2.77B. Remove the ratio framing.
MISLEADING: 'untested in Malaysian courts' — Malaysian common law follows English trust principles (Re Kayford 1975). Trust position is more established than claimed.
UNVERIFIED: 28M users / RM48B volume / 120 cyber investigators / Sabah 35% vs Klang Valley 78% — all need primary sourcing or removal.
omitted_facts: BNM 2025 Annual Report: e-payment transactions grew 25% to 18.4 billion in 2025 (strong official data)
Average e-money transaction size rose to RM43 in 2025 from RM33 in 2024
eMADANI actual criteria: STR recipients or income ≤RM100K per IRB records as of 15 July 2023
BNM does impose minimum capital funds on e-money issuers (8% of outstanding liabilities in earlier policy)
Consumer Credit Act 2025: Part V only from 1 June 2026
DOSM 2025 elderly figure is ~8% aged 65+, not 12%
overall_assessment: Core thesis directionally sound — PIDM non-coverage, trust account safeguarding, subsidy timing, FMOS launch all verified. But several sharp supporting claims fail strict fact-check: the 28M/RM48B market framing, fraud-recovery denominator mismatch, 'no capital buffer' claim (INCORRECT), eMADANI exclusion logic (INCORRECT), elderly 12% figure (INCORRECT), and overstated international comparisons. Strong advocacy draft but needs revision before publication.
